prose.overview: Artitaya Country Club — formerly Bangkok Royal Country Club — is a 36-hole resort complex in Ongkharak, Nakhon Nayok province, about 75–90 km northeast of Bangkok. Designed by Jack Tuthill (who has credits at courses including Kemper Lakes in the USA), the 18-hole championship course opened in 1994 and offers one of the most extraordinary value propositions in the Bangkok golf market: green fees of just 400 THB on weekdays and 800 THB on weekends for a 7,054-yard, par-72 layout. These prices appear remarkably low even by Thai standards and likely exclude caddie and cart — be sure to confirm the all-in cost when booking. The resort is Korean-owned and approximately 90% of its guests are Korean nationals, which is worth factoring into expectations around pace of play.
prose.layout_and_experience: The championship course stretches 7,054 yards from the blue tees — considerable length by any standard — laid out across flat parkland terrain with a well-balanced mix of water hazards and bunkers on most holes. Long, wide fairways are the rule, with strategic placement of ponds and lakes requiring accurate approach shots rather than penalty-style positioning. The greens are described as fast, which combined with the length makes the course a test even for low handicappers. The resort also features an Artitaya Golf Resort 36-hole section with a similar character. On-site facilities include accommodation, spa, massage, restaurants, and night golf capability — making it viable as a multi-day golf destination outside the city.
prose.tips: Confirm the total all-in price (green fee plus mandatory caddie and cart) before booking, as published green fees of 400–800 THB may exclude significant additional charges. The course is predominantly patronised by Korean golfers; if slow pace of play is a concern, aim for mid-week mornings. The resort is about 75 minutes by road from central Bangkok — plan an early departure. Night golf is available, which is a useful escape from midday heat. Club rental is available on-site, though specific brands and fees are best confirmed directly with the resort.
prose.location_and_access: Artitaya Country Club is located at 16 Moo 10, Rangsit-Ongkarak Road (Klong 14, Km. 9), Ongkharak, Nakhon Nayok 26120. Nakhon Nayok is northeast of Bangkok, accessible via Highway 305 (Rangsit–Nakhon Nayok Road). The drive from central Bangkok is approximately 90 km and 75 minutes by car in reasonable traffic. There is no public transport serving the course; a private car, rental, or taxi is the only practical option. The scenic countryside setting near the foothills of Nakhon Nayok makes the drive pleasant.
prose.rental_cta_context: Artitaya's extraordinary low green fee makes it easy to upgrade your equipment experience — pairing a budget green fee with LENGOLF's premium rental clubs from Bangkok is one of the smartest golf deals available in the region. LENGOLF delivers to your Bangkok hotel the night before.
